company_type: Oil and Gas Exploration and Production

dependencies: Availability of gathering, transportation and processing facilities
Third-party systems and facilities for oil, natural gas and NGL delivery
Water availability for hydraulic fracturing operations
Drilling rigs, completion crews and related equipment
risks: Lack of available capacity on gathering systems and pipelines could reduce prices or result in shut-in of producing wells
Third-party systems may be unavailable due to market conditions or mechanical reasons
Construction of new pipelines and infrastructure may be slow
Constrained pipeline capacity and oversupply in certain geographic areas resulting in negative spot market pricing (e.g., Waha Hub in Permian Basin)
Basis differentials becoming more divergent due to constrained pipeline capacity
Water sourcing limitations or restrictions could impact operations
Produced water disposal restrictions or moratoriums in areas of seismicity
Availability and cost of drilling rigs, fracturing crews and equipment
Electric grid outages impacting electric fracturing systems

regulatory_risks: Federal, state and local laws and regulations related to oil and gas development and hydraulic fracturing
Environmental regulations including Clean Water Act, Clean Air Act, Safe Drinking Water Act, CERCLA (Superfund), Oil Pollution Act
Endangered Species Act restrictions on drilling activities
Greenhouse gas and climate change regulations including EPA methane rules and waste emission charges
State and local restrictions or bans on hydraulic fracturing
Water withdrawal, storage and use regulations
Air emissions control requirements including PSD and Title V permits
Potential repeal of tax incentives (percentage depletion, intangible drilling cost deductions)
FERC regulations on natural gas transportation and pipeline rates
Interstate Commerce Act regulations on crude oil and NGL transportation
Occupational Safety and Health Act requirements
Climate-related disclosure mandates (e.g., California climate disclosure laws)
Potential LNG export restrictions or limitations
Cross default and acceleration provisions in debt agreements

sanction_risks: Geopolitical disruptions such as war in Ukraine or conflict in the Middle East
Actions by OPEC+ members affecting oil supply and prices
Potential retaliatory tariffs impacting LNG exports (including China's LNG tariffs)
